description:
# Open question

The typed-link model needs revisiting under the greenfield 'no workflow enforcement in core' rule (reaffirmed resolving bl-dd3e: only hardcoded rejection is claim-on-already-claimed; all other sequence enforcement is plugin pre-invoke best-effort OR skill hinting, never a core guarantee).

## The tension
Today TWO link types ENFORCE state transitions in core:
- `gates`: blocks `bl close` while any open gates link exists ('parent can't close while gate child is open').
- `depends_on`: blocks `bl claim` of the dependent until the dep closes.
Both are core enforcement of a transition — exactly what greenfield pushes out of core.

The NON-enforcing links (`relates_to`, `supersedes`, `duplicates`, `replies_to`) are pure metadata and stay in core untouched — this topic is only about the enforcing pair.

## Scope — decide
1. Which links are pure-metadata core vs which enforce. (Leaning: keep all link TYPES as core metadata; move only the ENFORCEMENT out.)
2. Where the enforcing reject re-homes: a plugin `close.pre` / `claim.pre` reject (best-effort, can fail to reject) vs pure skill hinting. Destination semantics, not source — consistent with the §3 rule.
3. bl-2a77 SEAM (load-bearing): the forge-gated delivery variant creates a 'gate child' that must block close until the forge merges. That currently relies on gates-blocks-close being enforced. Under greenfield that enforcement becomes the forge plugin's `close.pre`. This topic and bl-2a77 must agree on where that reject lives (same handoff pattern as the cwd precondition already carried to bl-2a77).
4. Does the spec (bl-2e26) gain a new § for the link model? It currently has none.

## Settled inputs (carry, don't redecide)
- No core workflow enforcement; claim-on-already-claimed is the sole hardcoded reject (bl-2e26 §3 note + bl-dd3e resolution).
- Plugin pre-invoke rejects via non-zero exit (§8 worktree-first); they are best-effort (a plugin can fail to reject).

## Relation
Intersects bl-2a77 (forge gate-child reject). Informs bl-2e26 (may add a § links section). Design-only: resolution is a note on bl-2e26.
title: Topic: typed links & enforcing gates under the no-enforcement model
